title: Adding a Primary Suite to Your Scarsdale Home: What to Expect
excerpt: A primary suite addition gives you the space and privacy you need without leaving your neighborhood. Here's what the process looks like in Scarsdale.
content:
If you're thinking about adding a primary suite to your Scarsdale home, you're making a smart move. A dedicated master bedroom and bath upstairs—or wherever it fits your house—changes how you live. You get privacy. You get space. You don't have to move.

The challenge is getting it done right. Scarsdale has its own building department and permit process, just like every other Westchester municipality. That means paperwork, inspections, and code compliance. It's normal. It's also why you need someone who knows the local system inside and out.

Why a Design-Build Approach Works for Primary Suite Additions

When you work with a design-build firm in Westchester, your architect and general contractor are the same person—or they're working as one team. That matters for a primary suite addition. You don't have one person drawing plans and another person building them. You have continuity. You have accountability.

At House Architecture & Construction, Silvio M. Luca is both a licensed architect in New York and a licensed general contractor in Westchester. He runs every project personally. When he designs your addition, he's already thinking about how to build it. When he builds it, he's making sure the design works in reality. That kind of oversight prevents costly mistakes.

What Makes Scarsdale Different—And What Doesn't

Scarsdale is a beautiful community with homes that range from mid-century moderns to classic colonials. Your addition needs to feel like it belongs. That's design work. It's not something you can phone in.

The permit side is straightforward. You submit plans. The building department reviews them. You get approval or you address comments. It's the same process every town in Westchester follows. What changes is the design itself. A residential architect in Scarsdale needs to understand the neighborhood's character and the specific lot challenges you're working with.

From Planning to Move-In

A primary suite addition isn't quick. You're building new framing, running electrical and plumbing, installing HVAC, finishing walls. You're also coordinating with the Scarsdale building department throughout. Inspections happen at rough framing, rough mechanical, and final. Each one has to pass.

The timeline depends on scope. A simple second-story addition with a bedroom and bath takes months. A more complex project with structural work or foundation upgrades takes longer. Your architect and contractor should be clear about what's realistic before you sign on.

Home additions in Westchester are common. What matters is working with someone who has done them before—someone who understands local codes, can navigate permits smoothly, and won't disappear when unexpected issues show up.

If you're ready to explore a primary suite addition for your Scarsdale home, call us at (914) 224-7383 for a free consultation. We'll walk through your space, answer your questions, and help you understand what's possible.
